input.instructions:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S1-ahc001-atcoder-ad
Title: S1. AHC001 / AtCoder Ad

Problem:
Place many non-overlapping axis-aligned ad rectangles in a 10000 x 10000 square so each advertiser gets a rectangle containing its requested point and with area close to its requested target area.

Grading target:
Continuous score from advertiser satisfaction.
Higher is better.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

variables.child_selected_prompt_text:
Solve the question directly.
Keep the entire JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Do not narrate your reasoning outside the response field.
In the response field, use exactly two short lines encoded with \n:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>\nJUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>",
  "result_type": "summary"
}

orchestration.decision_notes: Synthesizing the child results into a final algorithmic approach for the spatial optimization problem.

Synthesis brief from the earlier child-creation step:
The parent agent will take the initial spatial partitions from the spatial_partitioner and pass the resulting bounding boxes and advertiser constraints to the area_optimizer. The final output will be the set of optimized rectangle coordinates (x1, y1, x2, y2) for each advertiser.

Child results already returned to you:
[
  {
    "node_id": "node_root_helper_01",
    "summary": "FINAL_ANSWER: A k-d tree or quadtree algorithm\nJUSTIFICATION: These algorithms recursively partition space based on point density."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "node_root_helper_02",
    "summary": "FINAL_ANSWER: Local optimization of rectangle dimensions\nJUSTIFICATION: Adjust dimensions to match target area within cell constraints."
  }
]

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S1-ahc001-atcoder-ad
Title: S1. AHC001 / AtCoder Ad

Problem:
Place many non-overlapping axis-aligned ad rectangles in a 10000 x 10000 square so each advertiser gets a rectangle containing its requested point and with area close to its requested target area.

Grading target:
Continuous score from advertiser satisfaction.
Higher is better.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

results.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: A two-stage spatial partitioning and local optimization approach: 1. Use a k-d tree or quadtree algorithm to recursively partition the 10000 x 10000 space into cells, ensuring each cell contains a subset of advertiser points and respects the total area constraints. 2. Within each partitioned cell, perform local optimization of rectangle dimensions to expand or contract the axis-aligned rectangles around their respective points to match their target areas as closely as possible without overlapping or exceeding cell boundaries.

JUSTIFICATION: The k-d tree/quadtree approach ensures efficient spatial distribution and prevents large-scale overlaps by managing point density, while local dimension optimization maximizes the satisfaction score by minimizing the delta between actual and target areas within the established spatial constraints.
